news.headline: Blink Charging CEO to speak at the 37th Annual Roth Conference on 3/17 in California. Mike Battaglia presenting at 9:00am PT/12:00pm ET.
news.summary: In Bowie, MD on March 14, 2025, Blink Charging Co. (NASDAQ: BLNK), a prominent international company specializing in electric vehicle (EV) charging solutions, shared that Mike Battaglia, the President and CEO, will be speaking at the 37th Annual Roth Conference in Dana Point, California. The event is scheduled for Monday, March 17, 2025, at 9:00 am PT / 12:00 noon ET.

news.headline: Europe approves Bristol Myers' Breyanzi cell therapy for patients with relapsed or refractory follicular lymphoma.
news.summary: The European Commission has granted approval to Bristol Myers Squibb & Co‘s Breyanzi (lisocabtagene maraleucel; liso-cel), a CD19-directed chimeric antigen receptor (CAR) T cell therapy. This approval is specifically for adult patients with relapsed or refractory follicular lymphoma (FL) who have undergone two or more lines of systemic therapy. The decision was supported by data from the global Phase 2 TRANSCEND FL study. For more details, the complete story can be found on Benzinga.com.

news.headline: Wall Street banker Catherine Flax will speak at #SuperReturnEnergyNA on investing in fossil fuels. #ZefiroMethaneCorp
news.summary: Catherine Flax, a seasoned Wall Street banker and commodities executive, was honored as the Most Influential Woman in European Investment Banking in 2012 by Financial News (Dow Jones). Ms. Flax is scheduled to participate in a panel discussion on investing in fossil fuels at the upcoming SuperReturn Energy North America event in Miami this Tuesday.

news.headline: Amazon stock sees a brief recovery but still set for sixth weekly decline. No such streak since mid-2022.
news.summary: For the sixth consecutive week, Amazon's stock has been on a decline, marking its longest stretch since mid-2022. Although there was a slight rebound recently, the overall trend remains negative. This consistent downward movement is notable for the company, indicating a challenging period for Amazon in the stock market.

news.summary: It is essential to recognize that market corrections do not always follow the same pattern. To assess the severity of a correction and react accordingly, keep an eye out for five critical checkpoints. By monitoring these factors, investors can better navigate periods of market volatility and uncertainty. The checklist outlined in the article from Investor's Business Daily provides valuable insights for those concerned about stock market corrections. By understanding and utilizing this checklist, investors can make informed decisions during turbulent market conditions.

news.headline: Bioretec Ltd received notification of a change in holdings by W&M GmbH according to Securities Market Act on 14 March 2025.
news.summary: On March 14, 2025, at 7:30 p.m. EET in Tampere, Finland, Bioretec Ltd made a company announcement. They have received a notification on the same day in compliance with the Securities Market Act. The notification pertains to the share and voting rights held by W&M GmbH. The details regarding the specific numbers of shares and votes were not disclosed in the announcement.

news.headline: Bitcoin has crossed $84,000, sparking discussions among traders. Multi-month range breaks suggest major trend shifts, impacting price action.
news.summary: Bitcoin (CRYPTO: BTC) surpassed the $84,000 mark on Friday, sparking discussions among traders and analysts about the implications of this bullish trend on pricing. Highlighting the significance of multi-month range breaks in technical analysis, well-known trader Ansem emphasized the key role such breaks play in signaling major shifts in trends. Market price movements typically unfold in three stages: uptrend, sideways range (characterized by accumulation or distribution), or downtrend. In bull markets, assets often break out of accumulation zones following robust movements. For more information, the complete story can be accessed on Benzinga.com.

news.headline: Inventors from Kalamazoo develop THE FIRST MATE cutting board for fishermen, making filleting easier. #InventHelp.
news.summary: In Pittsburgh on March 14, 2025, two inventors from Kalamazoo, Michigan, unveiled their new innovation, THE FIRST MATE, aimed at revolutionizing fish cleaning and filleting. The duo sought to enhance the fisherman's ability to secure a fish to the cutting board, leading to the creation of this groundbreaking product. This improved cutting board design promises to streamline the fish cleaning process, potentially changing the game for fishermen everywhere.

CVS weight management program shows 15% average weight loss, 26% cost saving for participants. 265,000 members from 2023-2024.
news.summary: CVS Health Inc (NYSE:CVS) disclosed data from the CVS Weight Management program, with participants achieving an average weight loss exceeding 15%. This included existing users of anti-obesity medication who nearly doubled their pre-program weight loss by collaborating with program clinicians on diet and lifestyle. The data comes from a cohort of 265,000 members spanning 2023-2024. The results showcase enhanced weight loss and high satisfaction with the program for CVS Caremark clients’ plan members attempting a lifestyle-first approach. Additionally, members receiving clinical support while on weight loss medication experienced improved clinical effectiveness and cost savings. For more details, refer to the full story on Benzinga.com.

Inventor creates handheld electronic tool for testing winch cables to detect flaws, defects, or weaknesses.
news.summary: In Pittsburgh on March 14, 2025, an inventor from Fort Wayne, Indiana, shared their inspiration behind creating a device aimed at aiding towing services and other industries in examining steel wire cables for imperfections. The inventor emphasized the critical need to identify flaws, defects, and weaknesses that could escape detection. Consequently, they introduced a groundbreaking product called THE CABLE.

news.headline: Bank of America Corp discloses 5.15% stake in Umicore through transparency notification.
Bitcoin faces uncertainty amid US tariff tensions and market volatility. Trump's policies impact global markets and traditional assets.
news.summary: In the first three months of Donald Trump's presidency, he imposed tariffs on Canada, Mexico, and China, leading to unexpected turmoil in US and global markets. The crypto market felt the impact, with volatility increasing. Former US Treasury Secretary Lawrence Summers estimated that the tariffs had already reduced the US stock market value by $2 trillion. Some experts believe that a trade war weakening the US dollar could benefit Bitcoin as investors may turn to cryptocurrencies as a safe haven. Despite initial optimism in the crypto community following Trump's election, Bitcoin's value has since dropped. Cumulative flows into Bitcoin Spot ETFs reached record highs post-Trump's victory, but recent concerns over a potential tariff war have caused outflows. Some traders believe the market will focus on fundamentals over tariff threats as a strategy. Trump's fiscal austerity program is seen as a more significant driver of crypto markets than tariff policies. The article emphasizes that tariffs are just a short-term influence on investor sentiment and not a fundamental condition for cryptocurrency markets.

news.headline: Jim Foley appointed as new brand president of Zoom Drain, with 20+ years experience, to enhance franchise network in US.
news.summary: Bringing more than twenty years of experience to the role, Foley has been tasked with enhancing and expanding Zoom Drain's extensive franchise system across the United States. The company, headquartered in Plymouth Meeting, Pennsylvania, shared the news on March 14, 2025, via a press release. Zoom Drain is recognized as the top provider of services related to drains and sewers.

news.headline: Autoliv board renews EMTN Programme for €3 billion, approved by Audit and Risk Committee.
news.summary: On March 12, 2025, Autoliv, Inc.'s Audit and Risk Committee approved the renewal of its guaranteed euro medium term note programme. The programme, valued at €3,000,000,000, was extended for one year. Autoliv, Inc. is listed on the New York Stock Exchange under the ticker symbol ALV and on the Stockholm Stock Exchange under the ticker symbol ALIVsdb.

Skylark Labs CEO Dr. Amarjot Singh wins Emerging Growth Leader award at 2025 Space and Defense Innovator Awards in Austin.
news.summary: In Austin, Texas on March 14, 2025, Dr. Amarjot Singh, CEO of Skylark Labs, was honored as the Emerging Growth Leader at the Space and Defense Innovator of the Year Awards for 2025. This recognition was revealed at the SDI Showcase & Reception in Austin.

news.headline: CA Resources (NYSE:CRC) faces permit challenges and oversupply risks per J.P. Morgan analyst; Q4 2024 EPS $0.91, revenue $877M (missing estimates). Stay cautious.
news.summary: Alejandra Magana, an analyst at J.P. Morgan, upheld a Neutral rating for California Resources Corporation (NYSE:CRC) with a target price set at $63. In the fourth quarter of 2024, California Resources reported an adjusted EPS of $0.91, which was below the estimated $0.97. Additionally, their revenue totaled $877 million, falling short of the expected $897.75 million. However, Magana acknowledged the company's strong performance in the quarter, highlighted by EBITDA surpassing projections, steady operations, and ongoing momentum in CCS volume agreements. Despite this, California Resources faces challenges in maintaining output due to various factors.

Butler National Corp's Q3 revenue rose by 12% to $21 million compared to $19 million in Q3 fiscal year 2024.
news.summary: Butler National Corporation (OTCQX:BUKS) reported a 12% increase in revenue, reaching $21 million for the third quarter of fiscal 2025 compared to $19 million for the same period in fiscal 2024. The company, based in Olathe, Kansas, specializes in aircraft modification, maintenance, repair, and overhaul (MRO) services, as well as gaming management. For the quarter ended January 31, 2025, the company's operating income was $4.026 million, up from $3.795 million in the previous year. Net income also saw a rise, reaching $3.378 million compared to $2.382 million in the same period last year. Total assets for the company amounted to $128.603 million. Over the nine months ended January 31, 2025, revenue totaled $62.363 million, operating income was at $12.416 million, and net income stood at $9.220 million. For further details, the complete article can be found on Benzinga.com.

news.headline: Teamsters drivers at US Foods in Livermore, Calif. ratify first contract with substantial pay increase and better health benefits.
news.summary: In Livermore, California, around 200 drivers who are members of Local 853 of the Teamsters union working at US Foods have approved a new contract as of March 14, 2025. This agreement guarantees them a significant pay raise and improved health care benefits.

Belgium gift card market to reach US$1.99 billion by 2025, growing at 8.2% annually, experiencing 8.9% CAGR 2020-2024.
news.summary: The market intelligence report "Belgium Gift Card and Incentive Card Market Intelligence and Future Growth Dynamics (Databook) - Q1 2025 Update" has been included in the offerings of ResearchAndMarkets.com. Predictions indicate that the gift card sector in Belgium is set to expand by 8.2% annually, reaching a value of US$1.99 billion by 2025. Between 2020-2024, the country's gift card market demonstrated strong growth, with a Compound Annual Growth Rate (CAGR) of 8.9%. This positive trend is expected to persist, with further growth anticipated.

news.headline: Glass Lewis endorses Palliser's proposal for Rio Tinto's DLC review at the AGM on April 3, 2025. The proposal aims for an independent, transparent review.
news.summary: In London, Palliser Capital received support from Glass Lewis for its resolution at the Annual General Meeting of Rio Tinto Plc (LSE:RIO) on April 3, 2025. Palliser advocates for Rio Tinto to undertake an objective and transparent review of merging its dual listed company structure into a unified Australian-based holding company. Their request stems from the belief that shareholders should have access to more than an internal assessment conducted behind closed doors.

news.headline: Sun Communities, Inc. declared a $0.94 quarterly distribution per share for Q1 2025, payable on April 15 to shareholders.
news.summary: Southfield, Michigan - Sun Communities, Inc. (NYSE: SUI), a real estate investment trust focusing on manufactured housing and recreational vehicle communities as well as marinas, shared that its Board of Directors has approved a $0.94 per share of common stock quarterly distribution for Q1 2025. This distribution will be paid on April 15, 2025, to shareholders holding shares as of March 31, 2025.
